---
title: "Lecture 15: Objects & Scope"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "February 18, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---


## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 15]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Objects & Scope]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 4 | Unit II: JavaScript Functions & Events<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Represent real data with JavaScript objects
- Read, update, and add object properties
- Define object methods
- Understand global, function, and block scope
- Compare `var`, `let`, and `const`
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Object Basics"}

[What Is an Object?]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Objects store key-value pairs:

```javascript
const student = {
  name: "Riya",
  rollNo: 42,
  isActive: true
};
```

::: {.fragment .body-text-m .topbr}
Think of objects as structured records.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Access Properties"}

[Accessing and Updating Properties]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|2|3|4|5"}
console.log(student.name);      // dot notation
console.log(student["rollNo"]); // bracket notation

student.city = "Noida";        // add property
student.isActive = false;       // update property
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Methods"}

[Object Methods]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```javascript
const user = {
  firstName: "Aarav",
  lastName: "Sharma",
  fullName() {
    return `${this.firstName} ${this.lastName}`;
  }
};

console.log(user.fullName());
```

- `this` refers to the current object in method calls

---

## {data-menu-title="Nested Objects"}

[Nested Objects and Arrays]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```javascript
const course = {
  code: "BMC201",
  instructor: { name: "Dr. Nag", dept: "MCA" },
  topics: ["HTML", "CSS", "JavaScript"]
};

console.log(course.instructor.name);
console.log(course.topics[2]);
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Scope Intro"}

[Understanding Scope]{.slide-title}

<hr>

Scope defines where variables are accessible:

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Global scope
- Function scope
- Block scope
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Var Let Const"}

[`var`, `let`, `const` Scope]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.javascript code-line-numbers="|1|3-6|8-9"}
var globalValue = 1;

if (true) {
  var x = 10;
  let y = 20;
  const z = 30;
}

console.log(x); // works (function/global scope)
console.log(y); // error (block scope)
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Scope Chain"}

[Scope Chain Example]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```javascript
const appName = "WebTech";

function outer() {
  const unit = "JavaScript";

  function inner() {
    console.log(appName, unit);
  }

  inner();
}

outer();
```

Inner scopes can access outer variables.

---

## {data-menu-title="Best Practices"}

[Best Practices]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Use `const` by default
- Use `let` for changing values
- Avoid `var` in modern code
- Keep object names meaningful
- Keep nested structures manageable
:::

---

## {#end-slide data-menu-title="End Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="page-center">
<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Questions?]{.white-text}
</div>
<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Next: Lecture 16 - Arrow Functions & Callbacks
</div>
</div>
